beginner.question: What does text-align do in CSS?
beginner.answer: text-align controls the horizontal alignment of inline content (text, inline elements) within a block container.

beginner.question: What are the common values of text-align?
beginner.answer: The common values are left, right, center, justify, start, and end.

beginner.question: How do you center text horizontally?
beginner.answer: Use text-align: center on the parent block element.

beginner.question: How do you center a block element horizontally?
beginner.answer: Use margin: 0 auto on the block element with an explicit width set.

beginner.question: What does vertical-align do?
beginner.answer: vertical-align controls the vertical alignment of inline, inline-block, and table-cell elements.

beginner.question: What are common vertical-align values?
beginner.answer: Common values are baseline (default), top, middle, bottom, sub, super, and text-top.

beginner.question: Does vertical-align work on block elements?
beginner.answer: No, vertical-align only works on inline, inline-block, and table-cell elements.

beginner.question: What is the default value of vertical-align?
How do you align text to the right?
beginner.answer: Use text-align: right on the container element.

beginner.question: What does text-align: justify do?
beginner.answer: It stretches text so that each line has equal width, creating straight edges on both left and right sides.

intermediate.question: How does margin: 0 auto center a block element?
intermediate.answer: margin: 0 auto sets left/right margins to auto, which makes the browser distribute remaining space equally on both sides, centering the element.

intermediate.question: What is required for margin: auto to work for centering?
intermediate.answer: The element must have an explicit width (or max-width) set, otherwise it will fill the full width.

intermediate.question: How do you vertically center a single line of text in a fixed-height container?
intermediate.answer: Set line-height equal to the container's height to vertically center the text.

intermediate.question: How do you center an absolutely positioned element?
intermediate.answer: Use top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%) to center an absolutely positioned element.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between place-items and place-content in Grid?
intermediate.answer: place-items aligns items within their cells; place-content aligns the entire grid within the container.

intermediate.question: How do you center an inline-block element horizontally?
intermediate.answer: Apply text-align: center to its parent block element.

intermediate.question: What does align-items: center do in Flexbox?
intermediate.answer: align-items: center centers flex items along the cross axis (vertical by default).

intermediate.question: What does justify-content: center do in Flexbox?
intermediate.answer: justify-content: center centers flex items along the main axis (horizontal by default).

intermediate.question: How do you center content both vertically and horizontally with Grid?
intermediate.answer: Use display: grid; place-items: center on the container.

intermediate.question: What does place-items: center shorthand include?
intermediate.answer: place-items: center is shorthand for align-items: center and justify-items: center.

advanced.question: How does text-align: justify affect accessibility?
advanced.answer: Justified text can create uneven word spacing (rivers), reducing readability for people with dyslexia or other reading disabilities.

advanced.question: What is the difference between text-align: start and text-align: left?
advanced.answer: text-align: start respects the writing direction (left in LTR, right in RTL), while left is always left regardless of direction.

advanced.question: How does vertical-align: baseline work for inline-block elements?
advanced.answer: The inline-block's last line box baseline aligns with the baseline of the parent's text, which can shift elements up or down.

advanced.question: Explain how the CSS Box Alignment spec unifies alignment across layout modes.
advanced.answer: Box Alignment defines shared properties (justify-content, align-items, etc.) that work consistently across Flexbox, Grid, and block layout.

advanced.question: How do you center an element of unknown size using absolute positioning?
advanced.answer: Use top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%) — this works regardless of the element's size.

advanced.question: What is the behavior of align-content: center in a flex container?
advanced.answer: align-content: center centers the entire multi-line content within the container along the cross axis, when there is extra space.

advanced.question: How does margin: auto differ from justify-content: center in Flexbox?
advanced.answer: margin: auto on a flex item absorbs all free space on its side, while justify-content distributes space among all items.

advanced.question: What alignment possibilities does place-self provide in Grid?
advanced.answer: place-self: center aligns a single grid item within its cell both vertically and horizontally, overriding the container's alignment.

advanced.question: How does text-align-last differ from text-align?
advanced.answer: text-align-last controls only the alignment of the last line of a block or a line before a forced line break.

advanced.question: What happens when both margin: auto and justify-content/align-items are set on the same flex item?
advanced.answer: Margin: auto takes priority and absorbs free space, overriding the container's justify-content or align-items for that item.
